currentFocus: Accepted wrapper diagnostic, state-slimming, and PatchBench runtime online-button guard source commits are integrated on main; the next developer slice is a PatchBench online-readiness presentation helper/model extraction.

publicMain.nextWriteRequirement: Before any later write, re-read live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main; exact hashes in state are accepted evidence, not standing future main truth.

acceptedWorkerCommits.summary: Added a warning-only WinUI validation-wrapper long-path diagnostic plus matching tooling/release docs.
Slimmed live state batons to concise current-state pointers and removed stale history/log detail.
Hardened PatchBench static tests against future runtime Host/Join, matchmaking, online-play, or netplay button labels.

currentTruth.patchBenchBoundary: The integrated PatchBench change is test-only. AppCore receipt construction, patch planning, safe-copy manifests/signatures, launch arguments, process decisions, patch catalog rows, byte data, music behavior, Host/Join availability, online readiness, runtime proof, and release packaging are unchanged.
currentTruth.validationWrapperBoundary: The WinUI primary-lane wrapper follow-up is accepted as warning-only diagnostics/docs; it does not change package scripts, product behavior, or validation command semantics.
currentTruth.publicPrimaryRepo: The public repo remains the primary collaboration repo for source, docs, tools, tests, RE notes, wave notes, state batons, agent reports, readiness notes, and compact proof summaries that are non-secret and non-payload.
currentTruth.releasePublicationStatus: No release, tag, package, signing, installer, announcement, hosted workflow, or publication action is part of this integration.
currentTruth.stateBatonShape: State files should stay concise current-state handoffs. Completed release-audit history, repeated command logs, and local campaign details belong in canonical docs/readiness notes or local reports, not live state.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, screenshots/frame dumps, raw proof logs, copied executables, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env* files, build output, package output, and local runtime caches stay out of git and release ZIPs; use LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY.md for local-only placement.

activeSlice.name: patchbench-online-readiness-presentation-helper
activeSlice.goal: Extract online-readiness presentation text from RenderOnlineMultiplayerReadiness and RenderOnlineCompanionSessionReadiness into a small presentation helper/model.
activeSlice.claimBoundary: presentation-only future slice; no AppCore/service behavior move, Host/Join enablement, online action enablement, release, runtime, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, original-BEA, patch-row, byte-patch, launch, safe-copy manifest/signature, music, gameplay, visual, rebuild, or no-noticeable-difference claim changes

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

validation.currentIntegration: Integration gates cover docs/repo safety, JSON parse, stale-path scan, WinUI build, focused and broader PatchBench tests, patch-engine safety, and primary-lane validation.
validation.nextSlice: Prefer the PatchBench online-readiness presentation helper/model extraction; keep it presentation-only and preserve all online non-claims.
